skill.name: minimax-music-gen
skill.description: Generates vocal songs, instrumental tracks, and cover versions using the MiniMax Music API through the mmx CLI.
skill.summary: Create MiniMax music tracks from prompts, lyrics, style controls, or reference audio.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and credential patterns. Review confirms the skill intentionally uses the mmx CLI, MiniMax API access, local output files, and a persisted API credential, but found no evidence of malicious exfiltration or prompt injection. The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives from prose, prompt examples, and command examples rather than cryptographic code.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to install and execute the mmx CLI, run music generation and cover commands, and launch local audio players. This is expected functionality, but it creates command execution and supply-chain exposure if prompts, paths, lyrics, or installed packages are not handled carefully.
medium_findings.recommendation: Publish with a warning that the skill runs local commands. Require user confirmation before installing packages, invoking mmx, reading local files, or starting playback.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly show mmx, npm, and playback commands. The commands are part of the stated MiniMax music workflow, so the risk is real but not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: API Credential Stored in Hidden Home Directory
medium_findings.description: The skill tells users to authenticate with a MiniMax API key and notes that credentials persist in a hidden JSON file under the home directory. This is a sensitive credential storage pattern, even though it appears to be normal CLI authentication.
medium_findings.recommendation: Document that the API key is stored locally, avoid displaying the key, and do not read or transmit the credential except through the official mmx authentication flow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path and credential purpose are stated directly. The context is legitimate authentication, but credential persistence remains a meaningful security consideration.

medium_findings.title: User-Supplied Audio Paths and URLs Passed to CLI
medium_findings.description: Cover mode accepts a local file path or URL for source audio and passes it to mmx. This enables intended cover generation, but it may read local files or fetch remote content selected by the user.
medium_findings.recommendation: Confirm the exact source path or URL with the user before use, reject unexpected schemes, and avoid accessing files outside user-approved locations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source path and URL workflow is explicit. The impact depends on how the runtime validates user input and what access the CLI has.

low_findings.title: Prompt Guide Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections in the prompt guide are Markdown templates and music prompt examples, not instructions to execute shell commands. No shell invocation or executable code is present in that file section.
low_findings.recommendation: No security change is required for the prompt guide examples. Keep them clearly documented as prompt text examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose and fenced prompt templates. They do not contain executable shell syntax or a command execution path.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance matches occur in descriptive music-generation text, Markdown examples, or ordinary CLI availability checks. No evidence found of cryptographic API use, hashing, password handling, or covert host enumeration.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss these static matches as false positives. Continue to review future changes for real crypto or host-inspection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found music prompt language and decision-tree documentation at the cited locations. The only command checks are availability checks for required tools.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local CLI Invocation with User-Derived Arguments
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill builds mmx music commands from prompts, lyrics, genres, moods, instruments, source audio, and output filenames. This is necessary for the skill but requires careful quoting and confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Use structured argument arrays or safe shell quoting when executing commands, and present parameters for user confirmation before running mmx.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command templates show user-controlled music parameters being passed to the CLI. The templates quote many fields, but runtime implementation details are outside the skill text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Local Credential File
dangerous_patterns.description: The MiniMax API key is stored by the CLI in a hidden credential JSON file in the user home directory. This is sensitive local state that marketplace users should understand before installing the skill.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Warn users about local credential persistence and avoid any workflow that reads, prints, copies, or uploads the credential file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly states that credentials are saved to the hidden JSON file. No evidence found that the skill itself exfiltrates the file.

content.value_statement: Music creators need fast ways to turn ideas, lyrics, and references into usable audio drafts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through MiniMax music generation with prompts, lyrics, covers, playback, and iteration.
actual_capabilities: Generates vocal songs from short descriptions or detailed prompts using the mmx CLI.
Creates instrumental tracks with genre, mood, instrument, BPM, key, and structure guidance.
Turns user-provided lyrics into generated songs with section markers and style controls.
Creates cover versions from approved local audio files or source audio URLs.
Saves generated audio to a local MiniMax music folder with timestamped filenames.
Supports preview, confirmation, playback detection, and iterative regeneration workflows.
limitations: Requires the mmx CLI, Node.js, and a MiniMax API key configured by the user.
Generation quality, latency, quota, and content filtering depend on the MiniMax service.
The skill does not play existing music files unless they are generated during the workflow.
Users must ensure they have rights to any lyrics, references, or source audio they provide.

use_cases.title: Draft Creator Music
use_cases.description: Generate quick intro, outro, background, or mood tracks for videos from a short creative brief.

use_cases.title: Develop Song Ideas
use_cases.description: Turn lyrics, themes, and style notes into vocal demos that can be reviewed and revised.

use_cases.title: Prototype Game Audio
use_cases.description: Create instrumental loops or scene music drafts with controlled mood, tempo, and instrumentation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Track
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request from a short idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a warm instrumental track for a calm morning routine. Use acoustic guitar, soft piano, and a relaxed tempo.

prompt_templates.title: Make Lyrics Into a Song
prompt_templates.scenario: User has lyrics and wants a vocal demo
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these lyrics into an emotional pop ballad. Keep the lyrics language unchanged, suggest a vocal style, and ask before generating.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Controlled Instrumental
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an instrumental cyberpunk chase theme at about 140 BPM with dark synth bass, tight percussion, and a tense bridge.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Cover Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced cover generation with review steps
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a legally safe cover-style version from my approved reference audio. Confirm the source, style, lyrics plan, and output settings before running mmx.

output_examples.input: Generate a dreamy instrumental for a travel vlog at sunset.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a localized preview with type, style, mood, instruments, and output path, then asks for confirmation before generation.

output_examples.input: Use my lyrics and make them a soulful R&B demo.
output_examples.output: The assistant formats the lyrics with song sections, suggests vocal and production notes, and prepares an mmx generation plan for approval.

output_examples.input: Create an acoustic cover from this approved audio file.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks the source path, asks for the target style, confirms usage rights, and prepares a cover generation workflow.

best_practices: Confirm every generation plan before running mmx or using paid API quota.
Use clear genre, mood, vocal, instrument, and structure details for better results.
Keep lyrics original and verify rights for any source audio used in cover mode.
anti_patterns: Do not run cover generation on copyrighted audio unless the user confirms appropriate rights.
Do not expose or print the MiniMax API key or local credential file contents.
Do not accept vague prompts when the user expects precise genre, tempo, or instrumentation.

faq.question: Does this skill require a MiniMax account?
faq.answer: Yes. The user needs a MiniMax API key and the mmx CLI must be authenticated before generation.

faq.question: Can it generate both songs and instrumentals?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports vocal songs, instrumental tracks, and cover-style generation workflows.

faq.question: Where are generated files saved?
faq.answer: The skill saves generated music under the local MiniMax music folder described in the skill instructions.

faq.question: Can users provide their own lyrics?
faq.answer: Yes. Users can provide lyrics directly, revise generated lyrics, or ask MiniMax to generate lyrics from a theme.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it runs local commands, uses network APIs, and stores credentials locally.

faq.question: Does it handle copyright automatically?
faq.answer: No. Users must provide original lyrics or confirm they have rights to any reference audio or source material.
